title: Advancing Breastfeeding Support in Arizona
authors_full: Jennie Bever, PhD, IBCLC; Kimberly Moore-Salas, IBCLC; *Rizwana Biviji, PhD, M.S. (*Presenting author)

summary.hook: This poster presents a policy translation effort to expand breastfeeding and lactation support in Arizona by addressing gaps in Medicaid coverage for outpatient lactation services.
summary.summary_30s: This poster presents a policy translation effort to expand breastfeeding and lactation support in Arizona by addressing gaps in Medicaid coverage for outpatient lactation services.

talktrack_2m.what_this_is_about: This poster presents a policy translation effort to expand breastfeeding and lactation support in Arizona by addressing gaps in Medicaid coverage for outpatient lactation...
talktrack_2m.what_they_did: Policy analysis \+ policy translation / advocacy workflow description (legislative lifecycle tracking). Not stated on poster (policy review yields “14 states” with explicit...
talktrack_2m.what_they_found: A comprehensive SPA review (Jan 2012–Dec 2024) identified 14 states with explicit Medicaid provisions for lactation coverage and reimbursement.
talktrack_2m.why_it_matters: Establishing Medicaid coverage (HB 2051) and provider certification infrastructure (HB 2072) are positioned as levers to expand access to lactation services.
talktrack_2m.one_smart_follow_up: In your SPA review, what counted as an “explicit provision” for lactation coverage and reimbursement?

key_numbers: Not stated on poster (policy review yields “14 states” with explicit provisions).
SPA review: Jan 2012–Dec 2024; legislative lifecycle: 2025–2026 with dated steps in Jan–Mar 2026.
Medicaid policy documents (State Plan Amendments) and Arizona legislative process (bills HB 2051 & HB 2072).
SPA review (2012–2024) found 14 states with explicit Medicaid lactation coverage/reimbursement provisions.
HB 2051 expands AHCCCS coverage of breastfeeding/lactation services across multiple settings.
takeaways: A comprehensive SPA review (Jan 2012–Dec 2024) identified 14 states with explicit Medicaid provisions for lactation coverage and reimbursement.
The poster states Arizona lacks formal Medicaid reimbursement mechanisms for outpatient lactation services.
Policy impact pathway (diagram): certification → provider recognition → Medicaid enrollment → reimbursement authority → expanded support → healthier families.
